tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urinalysis shows glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may suggest underlying renal or hepatic pathology. Recommend further nephrological evaluation.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
